zh.title: 以太坊/美元技术分析：震荡偏弱整理格局
zh.content:
当前ETH价格围绕1776附近运行，整体呈现窄幅震荡走势。多空力量均衡但略偏空头压制，短线缺乏明确突破方向，市场进入整理阶段。

MACD维持负值且动能柱收敛，显示下行动能减弱但仍未转强。价格略低于SMA20，短期均线形成压制，反弹持续性有限。

下方支撑关注1766及1750区域，上方阻力位于1780与1800整数关口。若放量突破SMA20则有望改善结构，否则仍以震荡为主。

交易上建议高抛低吸为主，激进者可在支撑附近轻仓做多，止损设于跌破1750。若跌破区间则顺势跟空，控制仓位风险。

en.title: Ethereum vs US Dollar Technical Analysis
en.content:
ETH/USD is showing a mildly consolidative structure after a modest 24-hour uptick of 0.34%, with price hovering around 1776.43. Despite the small gain, the broader intraday behavior suggests indecision, as candles remain tightly compressed between 1766 and 1779. The market is effectively ranging, with neither bulls nor bears establishing dominance. Price action sitting just under the 20-period SMA hints at a slightly fragile short-term trend.

Momentum indicators reinforce this neutral-to-soft bias. The MACD reading at -5.64940 indicates bearish momentum remains present, even if weakening. Without RSI confirmation, momentum confirmation is limited, but price trading below the SMA(20) at 1779.71 suggests sellers are still exerting mild pressure. The lack of signal line data for MACD reduces precision, yet the negative value still reflects downside skew in short-term momentum.

Immediate resistance is defined by the intraday high at 1779.25, closely aligned with the SMA(20) near 1779.71, forming a tight resistance cluster. A break above this zone could open a path toward 1800, where psychological selling may emerge. On the downside, initial support sits at 1766.74, with stronger structural support likely forming near 1755–1760 based on recent consolidation lows overall maintaining a well-defined intrarange structure.

Trading conditions favor range strategies rather than breakout positioning in the current environment. Long entries may be considered near support zones with tight stops below 1765, targeting the upper resistance band. Conversely, short setups near 1779–1780 could be viable if rejection signals appear. Until a decisive breakout occurs above 1800 or below 1765, risk management and patience remain essential for sustained profitability.
